creator: jaretbartsch@yahoo.ca
text:
The problem is that Horde Groupware /usr/ports/deskutils/horde-groupware
and /usr/ports/mail/horde-webmail are conflicting when certain ports that
are intended for Groupware are required (such as horde-imp).

When Horde Groupware is installed as well as horde-imp, it conflicts a
bunch of pear ports installed which are needed for Horde Groupware.

Horde Webmail doesn't offer the extended functionality to that of Horde
Groupware which includes imp and other ports, but if they are installed,
the ports for pear and Horde Groupware will not update because they complain
about installing files into the same paths as imp and other pear ports
used for Horde Groupware. This needs to be fixed so that Horde Groupware
can use these ports which have pear dependencies which in turn will also
not update if imp and other ports for Horde Groupware are installed.

One is a meta-port and one is not, but ports that should be usable between
both software ports shouldn't conflict. These same ports which can be used
in either the Webmail or Groupware should be useable by both ports not
just one. The only time they should conflict is if attempts to install
both Horde Webmail and Horde Groupware while the one of them is already installed.

How-To-Repeat: The problem is very easy to repeat. Simply install horde-webmail, all the
required pear ports, and update with portmaster or portupgrade whichever
you prefer. Then, deinstall it and attempt installation of horde-groupware.
Install imp and other ports the horde-groupware port should support.
Attempt a port update of horde-groupware and you fill find it does not
want to update the horde nor pear ports that are installed for horde-groupware.
